paper_title: Apertural characteristics of certain unilocular foraminifera: methods of study, nomenclature and taxonomic significance

methods.description: This method involves revealing the internal structures of unilocular foraminifera by cracking their tests in various planes. The process is crucial for understanding the internal morphology and taxonomic significance of these organisms.

steps.description: Crack the tests in various planes using a sewing machine needle ground and polished to a point or sharp curved blade.
steps.searchable_summary: Use a prepared needle to crack foraminifera tests in various planes

steps.description: Transfer the chosen test with a drop of water by brush into a small rectangular well cut in the surface of a piece of X-ray film.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer test into a well on X-ray film using water

steps.description: Manipulate the test with the needle so that it becomes jammed into a corner of the well and held there by the needle; apply slight additional pressure to crack the test.
steps.searchable_summary: Manipulate and crack the test using a needle

steps.description: Ensure water remains in the well and the test remains wet during the cracking process to prevent the halves from flying apart.
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain moisture to keep test halves together

steps.description: Carefully separate the two halves of a cracked test to avoid damage to internal structures.
Mount pieces of foraminifera test with a drop of water on negative film glued to a stub for examination.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount test pieces on film for examination

steps.description: Examine the mounted test pieces in a Jeol T20 Scanning Electron Microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine test pieces using a scanning electron microscope
